headlines.title: US Oil Drillers Continue to Add Rigs
headlines.summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 548, down 30 from this same time last year. headlines.title: US Hits Iran-Flagged Oil Tankers While Peace Talks Continue
headlines.summary: U.S. forces struck two empty Iranian-flagged oil tankers in the Gulf of Oman on Friday, escalating pressure on Tehran’s shipping network even as Washington says a potential deal to halt the war could be close. headlines.title: Iran Seizes Tanker Carrying Its Own Oil
headlines.summary: Iran has seized a tanker that appears to have been carrying its own oil, highlighting just how tangled its export system has become under sanctions and blockade. headlines.summary: Oil prices head for a 7% weekly loss as traders struggle to price in conflicting signals from U.S.-Iran talks and ongoing Middle East attacks. headlines.title: Iraq Denies U.S. Claim Deputy Minister Helped Iran’s Oil Sales
headlines.summary: Iraq’s Oil Ministry has denied that its deputy minister was involved in opaque sales of Iraqi oil mixed with Iranian crude for Iran’s benefit, as the U.S. alleged on Thursday when it sanctioned the senior official. headlines.title: Iran Seizes Sanctioned Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman
headlines.summary: The semi-official Tasnim news agency reported that Iran has seized an oil tanker, accusing it of "attempting to disrupt oil exports and the interests of the Iranian nation." Tasnim did not elaborate on the seizure, but Iran's official state news agency, the Islamic Republic News

headlines.title: First Mexican Fuel Oil Cargo in 9 Months Arrives in Asia
headlines.summary: The first fuel oil cargo from Mexico has just arrived in Asia, in a first such shipment in nine months, as high Asian prices draw supplies from other regions amid the shock supply loss from the Middle East. headlines.title: India's Inflation Accelerates as High Energy Prices Start to Bite
headlines.summary: Inflation in India, the world’s third-biggest crude oil importer, accelerated in April as the higher global oil and gas prices started to feed through consumer prices.
